test_cases.description: Verify UID extraction from user journal filenames per systemd conventions

expected.note: Tested patterns: user-1000.journal->UID 1000, system.journal->system (no UID), user-foo.journal->EINVAL, user-65535.journal->ENXIO, user@*.journal~->EREMOTE. No fixture file needed; tests filename string parsing only.

test_cases.description: Parse journal export text into fields; verify field count, field ordering, and EOF behavior

importer_data.description: journal-data/journal-1.txt from systemd v260.1 test fixtures

expected.note: Original systemd importer test checks a complete entry boundary; this manifest checks four key fields as an ordered subset. EOF behavior: journal_importer_process_data returns 0 on incomplete line, 1 on complete entry.

test_cases.description: Verify journal_importer_eof returns true only after complete entry is parsed

expected.note: EOF not set until journal_importer_process_data returns 1 (complete entry). Returns 0 on incomplete data until entry complete.

Expected match string: (((L3=ok OR L3=yes) OR ((L4_2=ok OR L4_2=yes) AND (L4_1=ok OR L4_1=yes))) AND ((TWO=two AND (ONE=two OR ONE=one)) OR (PIFF=paff AND (QUUX=yyyyy OR QUUX=xxxxx OR QUUX=mmmm) AND (HALLO= OR HALLO=WALDO) AND B=C\000D AND A=\001\002)))

test_cases.description: Verify sd_journal_add_match rejects invalid match strings

expected.note: Invalid patterns: 'foobar', 'foobar=waldo', '', '=', '=xxxxx' - all should return error

test_cases.description: Test sd_journal_open_directory with multiple journal files; verify SD_JOURNAL_FOREACH forwards and backwards

journal_dir.description: Directory of 7 compressed journal files from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: Open the committed no-rtc journal directory and verify forward and backward iteration across all 7 compressed journal files. Synthetic skip/seek behaviors from test-journal-stream.c and test-journal-interleaving.c are inventoried for later generated fixtures and are not asserted by this fixture-backed case.

test_cases.description: Verify sd_journal_query_unique enumerates unique values for a field

journal_dir.description: Directory of 7 compressed journal files from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: QUERY_UNIQUE returns all unique values for specified field name

test_cases.description: Verify cursor test and seek semantics

journal_dir.description: Directory of 7 compressed journal files from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: sd_journal_get_cursor returns a cursor string; sd_journal_test_cursor returns true for the current cursor and false for an invalid cursor; sd_journal_seek_cursor rejects invalid syntax and accepts both an existing cursor and a syntactically valid cursor that does not identify an existing entry; a missing-cursor seek must not stay on the original cursor or move before the requested realtime

test_cases.description: Verify journal_file_verify correctly validates FSS-sealed journals

expected.note: With valid FSS key: PASS. Without key or wrong key: FAIL or ERROR. journal_file_verify returns verification range [from, to]

test_cases.description: Corrupted journal file should fail verification with detectable error

corrupted_file.description: Truncated zstd frame corruption fixture from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/corrupted/

expected.note: Truncated zstd frame: verification or decompression should fail with error mentioning truncation or corruption. Based on upstream test-journal-verify.c bit-flip detection pattern.

test_cases.description: Verify zstd-compressed journal fixture can be decompressed and read

journal_file.description: Compressed system journal from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: The fixture is a zstd-compressed systemd journal. This test covers compressed fixture read/decompression, not SYSTEMD_JOURNAL_COMPRESS writer algorithm selection or SYSTEMD_JOURNAL_COMPACT offset format variants.

test_cases.description: Corrupted journal file should not crash on reopen; write after corruption returns error gracefully

corrupted_file.description: Truncated zstd frame corruption fixture from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/corrupted/

afl_corrupted_1.description: AFL fuzz-generated corrupted journal from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/corrupted/

afl_corrupted_2.description: AFL fuzz-generated corrupted journal from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/corrupted/

expected.note: Reopen after corruption: error returned gracefully, not crash. Write after corruption: error logged, no crash. Tests all 3 corrupted fixtures.

test_cases.description: Parse journal file header fields: signature, flags, state, and header size

journal_file.description: Compressed system journal from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: Header fields include signature (8 bytes 'LPKSHHRH'), compatible_flags (uint32LE), incompatible_flags (uint32LE), state (uint8), and header_size (uint64LE). systemd journal headers have no version field.

test_cases.description: Verify journalctl --list-boots returns boot entries with index, boot_id, first/last_entry timestamps

journal_dir.description: Directory of 7 compressed journal files from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: Expected 4 boots in no-rtc fixture; format: [{"index":-3,"boot_id":"...","first_entry":1666569600994371,"last_entry":1666584266223608},...]

test_cases.description: Verify journal export format matches systemd journal-export spec

journal_dir.description: Directory of 7 compressed journal files from systemd v260.1 test/test-journals/no-rtc/

expected.note: Export format: KEY=VALUE per line; __CURSOR= unique cursor per entry; __REALTIME_TIMESTAMP in microseconds
